subject: Sociology
title: Bullying as Social Process
description: One-minute skill: Bullying as Social Process.

hook.text: Bullying isn't just mean kids. It's a social system with bystanders, enablers, and unwritten rules of power.

buildup.text: Sociologists study bullying as a group process, not an individual pathology. The audience matters as much.

discovery.text: Bullying enforces social norms. Kids who are different — in looks, behavior, or identity — face the most.

twist.text: Anti-bullying programs that focus only on individuals miss the point. The social structure of the school enables it.

climax.text: Bullying is a mirror of adult society: hierarchies enforced through aggression, normalized by silent witnesses.

punchline.text: Bullying polices who belongs and who doesn't.

quiz.question: How do sociologists view bullying?
options: As a social process involving group dynamics
